prompt: Assess our PMO effectiveness. Project managers complain the PMO creates too much paperwork, executives say they do not get useful insights, and project success rates have not improved in 2 years.
assertions: Must evaluate PMO process efficiency and identify bureaucratic overhead
Must assess stakeholder satisfaction across both PM and executive segments
Must measure PMO value delivery against project success rate trends
Must recommend specific actions to reduce overhead while improving executive visibility

prompt: Our PMO was established 3 years ago as a controlling PMO. The organization has since adopted agile for most projects. Is our PMO model still appropriate?
assertions: Must evaluate the fit between a controlling PMO model and an agile delivery organization
Must assess whether PMO governance creates friction with agile team autonomy
Must recommend an alternative PMO model better suited to agile environments
Must provide a transition plan from controlling to the recommended model

prompt: Calculate the ROI of our PMO. We spend $2M annually on PMO operations supporting a $50M project portfolio. How do we know if this is justified?
assertions: Must calculate PMO cost as a percentage of portfolio budget and benchmark it
Must identify measurable value contributions of PMO services to project outcomes
Must assess whether the PMO prevents cost overruns or schedule delays that exceed its cost
Must recommend metrics for ongoing PMO ROI tracking

prompt: Survey our PMO stakeholders. We need to understand satisfaction levels across project managers, sponsors, and delivery teams to improve our services.
assertions: Must design a multi-dimensional satisfaction framework covering different PMO service areas
Must segment analysis by stakeholder group to identify differing needs and perceptions
Must identify specific pain points and dissatisfaction drivers with actionable root causes
Must provide an improvement action plan prioritized by stakeholder impact
